markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Numbing Tonic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4496)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Numbing tonic makes it easier to push through the pain of battle and shrug off otherwise debilitating blows. You gain the listed [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) when you drink the elixir, and again at the start of each of your turns for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sanguine Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4497)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You gain greatly increased blood production, filtering out contagions and boosting your endurance but causing your body to bloat with blood.

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Fortitude and Reflex saves. This bonus improves when you attempt a save against an effect that has the [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) trait, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait, or would give you the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition.

**Drawback** Whenever you take piercing or slashing damage, you take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1 (or +2 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2 (or +3 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3 (or +4 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead and your critical failures on such saves become failures instead.
name: Sanguine Mutagen

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sanguine Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4497)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You gain greatly increased blood production, filtering out contagions and boosting your endurance but causing your body to bloat with blood.

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Fortitude and Reflex saves. This bonus improves when you attempt a save against an effect that has the [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) trait, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait, or would give you the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition.

**Drawback** Whenever you take piercing or slashing damage, you take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1 (or +2 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2 (or +3 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3 (or +4 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead and your critical failures on such saves become failures instead.
name: Sanguine Mutagen (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stone Body Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4500)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mutagen calcifies your body, making it as hard as stone. 

**Benefit** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage (except bludgeoning) equal to the listed amount. 

**Drawback** Your Speeds are reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) and you take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stone Body Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 5 to physical damage (except bludgeoning) and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stone Body Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 5 to physical damage (except bludgeoning) and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stone Body Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 920 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 10 to physical damage (except bludgeoning) and the duration is 1 hour.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stone Body Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4500)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mutagen calcifies your body, making it as hard as stone. 

**Benefit** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage (except bludgeoning) equal to the listed amount. 

**Drawback** Your Speeds are reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) and you take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4501)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and Will saves. After any round where you don’t spend at least 1 action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), you’re [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +1, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +2, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +3, the bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

The bonus to rolls is +4, the bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vaccine grants a creature immunity to a specific strain of disease of a level equal to or less than the vaccine’s level, and a +2 item bonus on all saving throws against other strains of the same disease. For example, a vaccine could grant immunity to filth fever inflicted by [otyughs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=327) but would only grant a +2 saving throw bonus against filth fever inflicted by a [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162).

**Craft Requirements** Creating a vaccine requires a sample of the [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) in question.

**Special** A vaccine is the same rarity as the disease it’s designed to prevent, or as the creature who inflicts the disease if the disease itself doesn’t list a rarity.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemical Gauntlet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4504)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 62

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An alchemical gauntlet emits small alchemical detonations when it makes contact with a foe. As an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, you can place a bomb into a metal bracket near the wrist of the gauntlet. The bomb must be one that deals energy damage, such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3287), [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), [frost vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3293), or [thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288). The next three attacks made with the gauntlet deal 1d4 damage of the bomb’s damage type in addition to the gauntlet’s normal damage. If the second and third attacks aren’t all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb’s energy is wasted. These attacks never deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage or other special effects of the bomb and aren’t modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb’s effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alchemical Gauntlet

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63 **Price** 35 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This long cylinder is topped by a pair of brass sockets and a collection of polished pipes and tubes. A total of two vials of [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3287) must be loaded into the sockets at the base of the weapon and the tubes cleaned and primed. Properly loading the flamethrower in this way takes 1 minute. When the trigger on a loaded flamethrower is pulled, the alchemist’s fire is siphoned into the rifle and shot out of the muzzle in a line of fire. The damage dealt by a flamethrower is determined by the strength of the weakest alchemist’s fire loaded into the flamethrower. **Activate** Interact ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Requirements** The flamethrower is loaded; **Effect** You pull the trigger, expending both loaded alchemist’s fires to shoot a line of fire. Creatures in the area take fire damage based on the weakest alchemist’s fire loaded into the flamethrower, as noted below. Creatures that critically fail the basic Reflex save additionally take the listed persistent fire damage.
  • **Lesser Alchemist’s Fire:** The flamethrower deals 1d8 fire damage (DC 15 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) in a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). 1 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
  • **Moderate Alchemist’s Fire:** The flamethrower deals 2d8 fire damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save) in a 60-foot line. 2 persistent fire damage.
  • **Greater Alchemist’s Fire:** The flamethrower deals 6d8 fire damage (DC 28 basic Reflex save) in a 90-foot line. 3 persistent fire damage.
  • **Major Alchemist’s Fire:** The flamethrower deals 10d8 fire damage (DC 37 basic Reflex save) in a 120-foot line. 4 persistent fire damage.
name: Alchemist's Flamethrower

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bomb Coagulant Alembic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4506)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Additive 0" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=4" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This apparatus increases the viscosity of the reagents in alchemical bombs, to deadly effect. As a 10-minute activity that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, you can use a bomb coagulant alembic to distill the contents of one alchemical bomb that deals [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage into a stickier substance. After distilling, the bomb deals no splash damage but instead deals persistent damage equal to and of the same type as its original splash damage. If the bomb already deals [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), distilling increases that damage by the bomb’s original splash damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bomb Coagulant Alembic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bone Dreadnought Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4507)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Fortress Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of bone-based [fortress plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21) is a masterpiece of alchemical science. This armor has a receiver that can hold a single [lodestone bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1904), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of bone dreadnought plate can be activated to cause the bomb to shift numerous small plates and hinges, offering a wide variety of protections, granting you resistance to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), piercing, and slashing damage equal to the loaded lodestone bomb’s splash damage. These effects last for 20 minutes, but each time you’re hit by an attack that deals damage of one of these types, decrease the remaining duration by 1 minute. Once activated, the armor can’t be deactivated. The armor uses up the lodestone bomb during the duration and can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bone Dreadnought Plate

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Collar of the Shifting Spider](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4508)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 133 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intimidating collar contains a hollow central tube and ends in twin metal points shaped like [spider](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=474) fangs. The collar can be filled with an alchemical mutagen as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. As a free action triggered when you roll initiative, you can activate the collar, causing the metal points to dig into your neck, inflicting 1 piercing damage and injecting the mutagen directly into your bloodstream. This has the same effect as if you drank the mutagen conventionally, except the duration of the mutagen is halved due to the more direct administration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Collar of the Shifting Spider

    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dread Helm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4509)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The faceplate of a dread helm has a fierce visage that magnifies the effects of fear. Cleverly concealed within is a reservoir that can hold a single [dread ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3292), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded dread helm can be activated to atomize the dread ampoule, creating a fear‑inducing mist that hangs around your face. The mist grants an item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks equal to the dread ampoule’s item bonus. The mist also deals mental damage equal to the dread ampoule’s splash damage to all creatures within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) other than you. The effects last for 3 rounds, consuming the dread ampoule, and once the atomization process has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dread Helm

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a non-injection melee weapon that deals piercing damage

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This reservoir and spring-loaded needle can be attached to a weapon to let it inject deadly poisons. Additionally, the reservoir can be filled with an injury poison. Immediately after a successful attack with the adjusted weapon, you can inject the target with the loaded poison by activating the reservoir with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. Refilling the reservoir with a new poison requires 3 Interact actions and uses both hands. 

Adding an injection reservoir to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) weapon).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Injection Reservoir

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living Leaf Weave](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of [leaf weave armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=28) is specially modified to metabolize the alchemical accelerants in medicinal compounds. A special receptacle in the armor can hold an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of living leaf weave can be activated to grow slithering vines from the armor, granting an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to Grapple, to your Fortitude DC to resist [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) attempts, and to your Reflex DCs to resist Trip attempts. The effects last for 3 rounds, consuming the elixir of life, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Living Leaf Weave

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetic Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4512)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Copper rings spiral around this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19). Twin electrical probes near the grip can socket into a jar of moderate (or higher leveled) [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded magnetic shield can be activated to convert it into an electromagnet. When an activated magnetic shield is raised, the circumstance bonus increases by 1 against attacks made with weapons primarily made of metal. If you use a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) against a creature attacking you with such a weapon, you also gain a +1 item bonus to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) attempts against that weapon until the end of your next turn. The shield remains charged for 3 rounds, consuming the bottled lightning, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnetic Shield

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Skin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4513)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41) has been treated with extract from [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), which can be reactivated in the presence of a strong acid. A receptacle in the armor can hold an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of ooze skin can be activated to cause the leather to weep slippery protoplasm, granting an item bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) checks equal to the acid flask’s item bonus. The protoplasm also irritates the skin on prolonged contact, causing any creature that [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [swallows](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) you to take acid damage equal to the acid flask’s splash damage. Ooze skin remains activated for a number of rounds equal to the level of the acid flask installed. The effects consume the acid flask, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ooze Skin

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Powered Full Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4515)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 65

</row>

</column>

---

Stasian actuators help the limbs of this [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) move of their own accord, as long as they’re supplied with power. A chamber in the chest plate can hold a single [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of powered full plate can be activated to lower the armor’s Strength requirement by 1 plus the item bonus of the loaded bomb. The armor’s normal penalties still apply, based on this altered Strength. However, add the bottled lightning’s item bonus to your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). These effects last for 10 minutes. The armor uses up the bottled lightning during the duration and can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Powered Full Plate

This trigger array uses [thunderstones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288) and crystals to remotely detonate alchemical bombs through harmonic vibrations. When a switch on the trigger is flipped up, the remote trigger begins to emit infrasonic pulses that slowly build up over the course of 30 minutes in up to three alchemical bombs placed next to the trigger (the trigger can be left to attune during your daily preparations). You can later activate the trigger to detonate one or more of the attuned bombs as long as they’re within 60 feet. Bombs detonated in this way deal their [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage as if they had missed a target in the square where they were triggered.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Remote Trigger

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 65 **Price** 450 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- This metallic tube has a complex array of lenses and prisms at one end and a hatch at the other. The hatch can be unlocked, loaded with a [sunrod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3355), and refastened using 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. A loaded sun dazzler can be activated to burn the sunrod to dust in a single focused flash, creating a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of scintillating light. All creatures in the cone must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save, with the following effects. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round or until it spends an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to rub its eyes, ending the blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round.
name: Sun Dazzler

This portable nozzle-and-trigger assembly based on spider spinnerets can extrude and weave alchemical adhesives into temporary constructions. As an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, you can attach a [tanglefoot bag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3295) to the extruder. A loaded tanglefoot extruder can be activated to consume the attached tanglefoot bag and convert it into a 30-foot rope, whip, or net, depending on the nozzle die you choose when activating the device. The created object lasts for 1 hour. The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) a created rope (if used to bind a creature) or net is equal to the consumed tanglefoot bag’s DC and Escaping destroys the created object.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Troll Hide](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4519)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 65

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor 

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tissue from a living troll has been integrated through this [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44). This armor has two organic receptacles on its back that can each hold a single elixir of life. One elixir takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. For the armor to function properly, each elixir must be the same level. A loaded suit of troll hide can be activated, causing regenerating tissue from the armor to fill your wounds. At the start of each round, you regain Hit Points equal to the level of the loaded elixirs. Each time you regain at least 13 Hit Points from the armor, you regrow one damaged or ruined organ. During a round that you regain 9 or more Hit Points from the armor, you can reattach severed body parts by spending an Interact action to hold the body part to the area it was severed from. If you take [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage, the armor deactivates until the end of your next turn. In the event the armor itself is damaged, it will restore its own Hit Points before it resumes healing you. The regeneration lasts for 8 rounds. Once activated, the armor can’t be deactivated. The armor uses up the elixirs of life during the duration and can’t be activated again until two new ones are installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Troll Hide

>
[Weapon Siphon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4520)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 65

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a melle weapon

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of tubing snakes down the striking surface of a weapon to deliver alchemical explosives. A single lesser alchemical bomb can be fitted to the weapon siphon as an Interact action. The bomb must be one that deals energy damage, such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3287), [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), [frost vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3293), or [thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288). The next three attacks made with the weapon deal 1d4 damage of the bomb’s damage type in addition to the weapon’s normal damage. If the second and third attacks aren’t all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb’s energy is wasted. These attacks never deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage or other special effects of the bomb and aren’t modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb’s effect.

Adding a weapon siphon to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) weapon).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon Siphon

---

Kept as a closely guarded secret by the deadly members of the [Red Mantis assassins](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=271), this poison is treated with reverence for its ability to end lives. If the victim dies while affected by this poison, its body decomposes to nothing in 1 minute, leaving only its gear behind. Non-magical preservation can’t protect the tainted corpse. [_Gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625) works on the poisoned body only if cast as a 5th-level spell and the caster succeeds at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check against the poison’s saving throw DC when casting the spell. Even if cast successfully, _gentle repose_ only works as if it had been cast at 2nd level.  

**Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d12 poison damage and [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 9d12 poison damage and doomed 2 (1 round) **Stage 3** 11d12 poison damage and doomed 3 (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Each round the type of damage dealt by this poison changes, starting with [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), then [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), then cold, and so on. This oily, dark-purple powder gives off the distinct odor of boiled leather. When delivered, the poison acts quickly to constrict the victim’s blood flow to their extremities and turn their lungs into a soft jelly. A victim of astringent venom is recognizable by the frostbite-like hue of their hands as they loose circulation to their extremities, making it difficult for them to hold things. Each round at the beginning of their turn, a creature affected by astringent venom must succeed at a [flat DC 5 check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or drop one random item they’re holding.

Sometimes called liquid persuasion, this sweet-tasting tincture induces euphoria that lowers inhibitions and increases trust. The status penalty from being [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) due to this poison doubles when applied to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), and Perception DCs to detect a Lie. 

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 10 minutes; **Stage 1** stupefied 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** stupefied 2 (1 minute); **Stage 3** stupefied 3, and the victim’s attitude toward others improves by one step (1 minute)

This poison is made from toxic spores reduced to a gray powder and suffused with necromantic energy. If a creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points while under the effect of [_death knell_](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) powder, it must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or die (this is a [death effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331)). If a creature dies from _death knell_ powder, a random creature adjacent to the victim is bolstered by its departing life force and gains 20 [temporary HP](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 10 minutes.

**Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 9d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 12d6 poison damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />

**Bulk** L

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

---

This poison draws power from the magic of its victim. If a creature under the effects of eldritch flare [Casts a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), excess magical energy feeds back into the toxin, increasing the DC of the poison by 2 that round. In addition, if the spell deals damage, the poison deals half its damage as one of the types of damage the spell deals (the other half remains poison damage). If the target is immune or has resistance to the spell’s damage, the poison deals half its damage as mental damage instead. If the victim casts no spells during a round while affected, the poison still deals its poison damage. 

**Saving Throw** DC 35 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 8d6 damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 10d6 damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 12d6 damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eldritch Flare

Rendered from the fat of corporeal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures and infused with ghoulish magic, this yellowish oil causes its victims to experience stabbing hunger pangs that only living flesh can abate. If the victim eats at least a mouthful of humanoid flesh, it ignores the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) condition from _hunger oil_ for 1 minute. Victims under the effect of _hunger oil_ regain only half as many Hit Points from healing effects unless they’ve eaten at least a mouthful of humanoid flesh in the last minute.

**Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** enfeebled 2 (1 minute); **Stage 2** enfeebled 3 (1 minute); **Stage 3** enfeebled 4 (1d4 minutes)

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of [_ghoulish cravings_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1996).

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 69

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick green cream quickly seeps into victim’s skin. In addition to causing painful swelling in the brain, liar’s demise compels the victim to speak only the truth. While under the effect of liar’s demise, the victim takes the listed poison damage and mental damage for any time they voluntarily and knowingly tell a lie, due to the poison’s increased blood pressure to their brain. They take this damage once per round at most, even if they lie several times in rapid succession. The victim is aware of this effect and can choose to not answer or give only evasive, technically truthful, answers—this is a mental effect. 

**Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1, 3d6 poison damage and 3d6 mental damage for lying (1 minute); **Stage 2** stupefied 2, 4d6 poison damage and 4d6 mental damage for lying (1 minute); **Stage 3** stupefied 3, 5d6 poison damage and 5d6 mental damage for lying, and the damage becomes a [death effec](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331)t. If it reduces the victim to 0 Hit Points, the victim’s head explodes, causing death (1 minute).

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of [_zone of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Liar's Demise

The poison known as looter’s lethargy ensures no thieves are strong enough to walk off with pilfered treasures. Commonly smeared on locks, chests, and even valuable items themselves, the poison slowly saps the strength of those who touch it. Nearby guardians can then simply follow the resulting trail of discarded valuables to find the weakened trespasser.

**Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 1 hour; **Stage 1** reduce Bulk limit by 3 (1 minute); **Stage 2** [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), reduce Bulk limit by 4 (10 minutes); **Stage 3** flat-footed, reduce Bulk limit by 5 (10 minutes)

</column>

Pale fade is a white ointment with a sharp, earthy scent. The poison rapidly desiccates flesh, which then crumbles and forms a cloud of pallid dust. If the victim is concealed by this poison, then the cloud of dust also conceals other creatures from the victim.

**Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 10d6 poison damage and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 12d6 poison damage, drained 1, and [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) (1 round); **Stage 3** 15d6 poison damage, drained 1, and concealed (1 round)

>
[Pummel-Growth Toxin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4544)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 70

**Price** 550 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This substance is the result of a failed alchemical experiment to regrow a severed arm. An extra outsized, uncontrolled limb of the sort used for manipulation grows from the victim’s body. The limb initially flails about, throwing the creature off-balance. Once it “matures,” the limb pummels the victim instead. The limb can’t deal its bludgeoning damage if the victim is unable to take actions. Upon recovery from the poison, the extra limb withers and falls off. 

**Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage, [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, and must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to perform an action with the manipulate trait or the action fails and is lost (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1, 2d6 bludgeoning damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, slowed 2, 4d6 bludgeoning damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pummel-Growth Toxin

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />

---

Derived from a mixture of krooth venom and an agitating agent, scarlet mist causes bleeding that turns into a foaming mist. When a creature under the effects of scarlet mist takes persistent bleed damage from the toxin, creatures within 5 feet take the splash damage. The persistent damage can’t be staunched until the poison’s effects end. A creature that can’t bleed doesn’t take the bleed damage or cause the splash damage but can still take the poison damage. 

**Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage, 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and 1 poison [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage, 2d4 persistent bleed damage, and 2 poison splash damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage, 3d4 persistent bleed damage, and 3 poison splash damage (1 round)

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sight-Theft Grift](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4546)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The clause of _sight-theft grift_ which states that “the blinded condition from this poison lasts for an additional 24 hours once the poison has run its course” only applies if the target reaches Stage 3 and is blinded._

**Price** 115 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blackish grit seems to absorb light and resembles particularly dark flakes of black pepper. Infused with the blindness spell, sight-theft grit causes the victim’s sight to dim and then depart altogether. The blinded condition from this poison lasts for an additional 24 hours once the poison has run its course. 

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 14 hours; **Stage 1** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and a –2 status penalty to visual Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 2** dazzled and a –4 status penalty to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks (1 hour); **Stage 3** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) (2d6 hours)

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sight-Theft Grift

[Smother Shroud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4547)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Smother shroud robs a victim of distinguishing features, making it difficult for anyone to identify the corpse. Swelling and distention of facial features makes the victim unrecognizable. Increase the DC of any checks made to identify a creature under the effects of smother shroud by twice the stage of the poison. If the victim dies while under the effects of this poison, its corpse retains an inability to take actions with the auditory trait, and if it tries to speak and fails, it counts against responses to the talking corpse spell. 

**Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d4 poison damage, dazzled, a –4 status penalty to Perception checks to hear and smell, and must succeed at a DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to take actions with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait or the action is lost (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d4 poison damage, blinded, deafened, unable to smell, unable to take actions with the auditory trait, and unable to breathe

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Taster's Folly](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4550)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 71

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Devised to bypass detection, a dose of taster’s folly consists of two compounds that aren’t mixed but placed in the contents of one meal. Each compound is harmless on its own. The DC to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about this poison from one of its components is 23 and attempts to use magic to detect the unmixed components require a successful DC 23 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check. The onset period begins only if a victim consumes both compounds during the same hour. If the two compounds mix prior to consumption, they become toxic and are detectable as such. The [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) condition can’t be ended until the poison’s effects end.

**Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage (1 minute); **Stage 2** 3d4 poison damage and sickened 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 4d4 poison damage and sickened 1 (1 minute)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Taster's Folly

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unending Itch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4551)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=162" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 71

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Invented to cause a lengthy and unpleasant demise, this poison manifests as an itch that can’t be soothed. The victim experiences the poison damage as irritation rather than pain or sickness and must succeed at a DC 34 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check to realize they’re poisoned. The poison can also be identified with a DC 34 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check. Once the victim has lost half or more of its Hit Points, the DC drops to 30 for either check. As long as the victim doesn’t realize it’s poisoned, the GM makes its saving throws in secret.

**Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude (secret); **Maximum Duration** 5 minutes; **Stages 1–5** 1d6 poison damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unending Itch

The delicate process of extracting violet venom from a violet fungus leaves it diluted at the best of times. Alchemists are still on the hunt for a truly pure, unadulterated version of this highly toxic poison.

Warpwobble poison causes hallucinations of space bending and stretching, leading to vertigo and an inability to discern a stable place to move.

>
[Bralani Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4554)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Bralani breath_ is a species of mint with small, green flowers, said to grow on battlefields where the opposing sides were equally matched. Sprigs of the flowers blow gently in a breeze of their own creation. If you cast [_mage armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611) using _bralani breath_, you release a small blast of concussive air in an emanation of a size that depends on the catalyst’s type. Some unattended objects are pushed away from you. Large or smaller creatures must succeed at a Fortitude save equal to your spell save DC or be pushed the same distance away from you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Price** 55 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Cast a Spell --- Dragon scales of all sorts can be powerful magical reagents, infusing magic with the might of their breath. When used to cast [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), a scale from a _dragon’s throat_ causes the spell to lose the force trait and damage type. Instead, the spell has the damage type of the breath weapon belonging to the dragon the scale came from, along with that trait. Damage types are associated with the following dragons, with uncommon dragons noted with an asterisk. **Acid**: [Black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636)*, [copper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=644)
**Cold:** [Silver](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=645), [white](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647)
**Electricity:** [Blue](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=653), [bronze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=648), [cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=637)*, [sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=650)*
**Fire**: [Brass](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589), [gold](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=658), [magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=643)*, [red](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=582), [underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=657)*
**Mental:** [Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=652)*
**Negative**: [Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=656)*
**Poison:** [Green](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=401)
name: Dragon Throat Scale

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Cast a Spell --- This catalyst is made from twisted sapling bark harvested under a full moon. When used to cast a [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) spell, the enchantment creates a blissful experience for one target of your choice. When the spell ends, even if you Dismiss it, the sudden mental dissonance between the charmed state and reality forces the target to attempt a Will save against your spell DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 or, if the spell ended because of a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is stunned 2 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is stunned 3 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 2 rounds. Euphoric Loop **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Price** 30 gp Euphoric Loop (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Price** 450 gp --- The catalyst affects all targets of the _charm_ spell, up to 10.
name: Euphoric Loop

Euphoric Loop **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Price** 30 gp Euphoric Loop (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74 **Price** 450 gp --- The catalyst affects all targets of the _charm_ spell, up to 10.

    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fearcracker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4557)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _fearcracker_ contains fragments of broken mirrors and reagents that pop and smoke when consumed. When thrown down in your space as part of a [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) spell, your and your images’ appearance twists nightmarishly. When an image is destroyed, it “dies” in a disturbing fashion, rendering the attacker [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. This aspect of the spell has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fearcracker

    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gravemist Taper](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4558)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 74

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _gravemist taper_ is a conical candle with symbols of terror and death carved into the wax. The taper can be used as a catalyst when casting an obscuring mist spell, burning the taper away, coloring the mist gray, and filling the mist with ghastly, shadowy shapes. The [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to overcome the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) state from the mist rises to 7, and a creature who fails such a check becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. This aspect of the spell has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gravemist Taper

_Nevercold_, sometimes mistakenly referred to as nevercoal, is the charcoal left after wildfires in the First World. True to its name, nevercold remains warm to the touch. If you use nevercold to cast [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529), the spell’s duration increases by 1 minute, the cold resistance you gain from it lasts 1 hour, and you’re protected from the effects of severe cold for 8 hours.

Beloved by the church of the [Seventh Veil](/Deities.aspx?ID=358), a _seventh prism_ is a crystal that disperses its internal light, casting an aurora of color. If you use a seventh prism to cast [_color spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), targets are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for twice as long as their saving throw indicates. On a critical failure, the target is dazzled for 1 minute after its [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) condition ends. Motes of shifting rainbow hues cloud the eyes, making it difficult to see details.

</title>

</row>

</column>

---

Beloved by the church of the [Seventh Veil](/Deities.aspx?ID=358), a _seventh prism_ is a crystal that disperses its internal light, casting an aurora of color. If you use a seventh prism to cast [_color spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), targets are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for twice as long as their saving throw indicates. On a critical failure, the target is dazzled for 1 minute after its [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) condition ends. Motes of shifting rainbow hues cloud the eyes, making it difficult to see details.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Anathema Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4562)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 76

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour

**Usage** affixed to the ground in four spaces along a circle with a 20-foot radius

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First used by [aasimars](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=68) of Tianjing to weaken [qlippoth](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=460), an _anathema fulu_ comes in four pieces, one placed in each cardinal direction. Choose one of the following traits when activating the fulus: [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). If a creature with that trait starts its turn in the area, it must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude saving throw or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 until the start of its next turn. On a critical success, the creature becomes temporarily immune to any _anathema fulus_ for 1 hour. Regardless of the save result, if you subsequently cast banishment on such a creature in the area, the creature takes the –2 penalty described in that spell for using a material component that’s anathema to that creature. The fulu acts as that component; you don’t need to add it. If a creature affected by this penalty rolls a saving throw against banishment, the caster can use a free action to force the result one step lower. Doing so burns the whole fulu out, ending the effect. If any of the fulu’s pieces are moved or destroyed after activation, the effect ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anathema Fulu

[Demolition Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4563)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to an object or structure

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _demolition fulu_ allows a saboteur or excavator to be far away from the scene when demolition happens. The fulu crumbles to ash over 5 minutes to 8 hours, as you determine when you place the fulu. Once the duration ends, the fulu lowers the Hardness of the object it’s affixed to by an amount equal to the fulu’s level and then deals the listed damage to the object. A _demolition fulu_ serves as a hazard with a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) DC to detect it and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC to disable it according to its type.

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes called the _moth fulu_, good for destroying softer objects; 3d6 bludgeoning damage, DC 17.

---

Sometimes called the _rust fulu_, good for destroying metal objects; 10d6 bludgeoning damage, DC 37.

Incorporating green in its writing, a _discord fulu_ is a popular but unethical tool often deployed on romantic rivals to foil their advances. The first failure the creature rolls on a Diplomacy check becomes a critical failure instead, and the fulu turns to ash, ending its effect.

---

Tradition says the _dragonfly fulu_ should be affixed to the upper back, like the wings of an insect. When you Activate this fulu, you gain a +2 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) for 1 minute. During this time, you can attempt an Athletics check to High Jump or Long Jump as a single action without the [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) requirement. You can also High Jump or Long Jump from a nonsolid substance, such as air or water, but if you use this power of the fulu, its effects ends after you jump.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonfly Fulu

The _escape fulu_ is a charm common among wealthy people, who wear the talisman in case of kidnapping. When you Activate this fulu, for 1 minute, you gain a +2 status bonus to your attempts to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as well as to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405).

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

The inscription on a _ghost courier fulu_ leaves space for a message, and a prominent red stamp indicates which _ghost delivery fulu_ this fulu homes in on (see below). When you Activate this fulu, you dictate a message up to 25 words long that then magically appears on the paper in the language you spoke. The fulu then disappears into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), arriving at the assigned _ghost delivery fulu_ in 2d10 hours, provided that fulu is within 500 miles. There, the fulu’s magic dissipates, but the message remains. If the fulu takes any damage in transit, it has a 50% chance to drop back into the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), intact but bereft of magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Courier Fulu

An enterprising chirurgeon reversed the forces evoked by a void [_thousand-pains fulu_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2044) to create the _golden breath fulu_, which fortifies ki and the body as elements move out of balance. When you Activate this fulu, you regain 75 Hit Points and attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end any [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) affecting you. The fulu is particularly appropriate help for ending any persistent damage. Also, if you would regain more Hit Points from the fulu than your maximum, you can gain the excess as [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) or distribute the excess among creatures of your choice within 30 feet. The temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

</column>

---

Some [inventors](/Classes.aspx?ID=19) in Tian Xia put fried snacks near their innovations, a charm to make devices behave as intended. The practice rubbed off on other inventors, who replaced the food with a drawing on an _inventor’s fulu_. When you Activate the fulu, your critical failure becomes a failure, and you can spend just 1 minute to return your innovation to full functionality. The fulu then burns up, and its effects end.

Despite the name, a _kaiju fulu_ protects a building against damage of all sorts. When an affixed structure no larger than 100 feet × 100 feet and up to 50 feet tall takes damage, the structure is as hard as [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) against that damage and any damage that occurs within 1 hour thereafter. The fulu then crumbles to dust, and its effects end.

</title>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />

---

A _mourner’s dawnlight fulu_ is a stack of pages that resembles joss paper, used to locate the remains of the lost. When you Activate the fulu, you envision a specific object or deceased creature you’re familiar with and want to find. You then rip the fulu into pieces and let them drift in the wind. If the item or creature you seek is within 500 feet, the pieces flutter through the air and land on the target, or on the surface closest to a buried or otherwise obscured target. If the torn fulu lands or fails to locate the desired target, its magic ends.

</column>

[Poracha Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4573)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

Folklore from near the Forest of Spirits tells of the origin of the _poracha fulu_. Once, a traveler saved an eight-legged feline who turned out to be a [poracha](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3143) prince. In return, the prince gave the traveler a fulu that later prevented a fast-acting poison from slaying them. Traditionally, one wears a string of up to nine poracha fulus, which counts as one talisman. Each time you take [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), one poracha fulu affixed to you negates the damage and crumbles to dust. This response is automatic, but you can use a free action (envision) to prevent your fulus from responding. If you do, any poracha fulus affixed to you never respond to that persistent damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Poracha Fulu

---

An acolyte accidentally left fulu paper outdoors overnight during a divine ceremony to [Tsukiyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=317) conducted only during a supermoon, creating the first _reflected moonlight fulu_. When you Activate this fulu, you reroll your saving throw against the triggering effect and take the better result. If this new roll is a critical success, the effect is reflected on its creator, who’s treated as the effect’s target, with any negative damage converted to positive. A reflected effect or spell affects only the original creator, even if it was an area spell or one that affects more than one creature.

</column>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 78

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to the ground

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>command, envision, interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _sentry fulu_ depicts an armed guard. When you activate the fulu, it takes the shape of a Tiny humanoid guard made of paper and keeps watch over an area in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). The guard has a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC of 20, along with precise visual senses and imprecise hearing and vibrational sense to detect creatures moving in its area, including through the air. You dictate whether the guard remains still in its area or patrols it; if the latter, you also determine the path the guard takes, at a Speed of 25 feet. You also determine a password others must give the guard to bypass it. If a creature enters the area without giving the password, the sentry creates either an audible or mental alarm. An audible alarm has the sound and volume of a human shouting, as well as the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait, allowing each creature that can hear it to attempt a DC 15 Perception check to wake up if they’re asleep. The mental alert reaches you if you’re within 60 feet of the active guard (see below). The guard remains active for 8 hours, and then the fulu is consumed.

If you have more than one _sentry fulu_, you can set up several that function as a unit, provided their areas touch or overlap. When one sounds its alarm, the alarm passes through all of them, so you must be within range of only one to hear it or receive the mental alert. Creatures that give the correct password to one _sentry fulu_ in a unit need not give that password again to the others. The destruction of one fulu-created guard in a unit sets off the alarm in others in connected areas.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sentry Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stumbling Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4576)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The [kitsune](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=38) who first created a _stumbling fulu_ advised the user to tuck the fulu under the target’s belt for maximum effect. When the creature to which the fulu is affixed completes a [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) action, the creature must attempt a DC 17 Reflex save. On a failure, some element of the armor the fulu is affixed to comes undone, making the wearer [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1. On a critical failure, the target falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and is clumsy 2. The clumsy condition remains until the target takes a total of 1 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, plus 1 additional Interact action per value of the clumsy condition above 1, to properly reclothe itself. Once the fulu activates, it burns up, its magic lasting only as long as the conditions it has imposed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stumbling Fulu
