<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Pains Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4577)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a creature

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by a [chirurgeon](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=6) who threw away morality in search of endless life, a _thousand-pains fulu_ blocks the natural flow of elements in the body. A creature to which the fulu is affixed must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save against damage determined by the fulu’s type. Failure or critical failure primes the target for [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) triggered by a specific condition that must be met within the fulu’s duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Burl)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Though this green fulu looks inviting, it has a foreboding aura. The DC is 35, and the fulu deals 10d8 poison damage, causing the target’s body to respond adversely to their ki. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes fire damage, it takes 2d8 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well. These effects have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Blade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This red fulu feels rigid, its edges sharp, and looking at it makes you feel feverish. The DC is 23, and the fulu deals 5d6 slashing damage as meridians burst and cut through flesh. On a failure or critical failure, if the target fails to take an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait on each of its turns, it takes 1d6 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 630 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Looking at this black fulu leaves you queasy and cold. The DC is 30, and the fulu deals 10d6 cold damage as the target’s ki warps and drains. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes damage from a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) effect or becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), it takes 2d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). These effects have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Needle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 270 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Picking up this white fulu feels like you’ve stabbed your hand with a pin. The DC is 27, and the fulu deals 7d6 piercing damage as hair, nails, and connective tissues stab inward. On a failure or critical failure, if the target then takes an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait, it takes 2d6 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Stone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This fulu’s yellow parchment feels dusty and stiff. The DC is 19, and the fulu deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage as muscles swell to crush joints and bones. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes electricity damage, it takes 1d6 [persistent bludgeoning damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 negative damage by dissipating the target’s ki. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well. These effects have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and negative traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Pains Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4577)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a creature

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by a [chirurgeon](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=6) who threw away morality in search of endless life, a _thousand-pains fulu_ blocks the natural flow of elements in the body. A creature to which the fulu is affixed must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save against damage determined by the fulu’s type. Failure or critical failure primes the target for [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) triggered by a specific condition that must be met within the fulu’s duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Burl)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Though this green fulu looks inviting, it has a foreboding aura. The DC is 35, and the fulu deals 10d8 poison damage, causing the target’s body to respond adversely to their ki. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes fire damage, it takes 2d8 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well. These effects have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Blade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This red fulu feels rigid, its edges sharp, and looking at it makes you feel feverish. The DC is 23, and the fulu deals 5d6 slashing damage as meridians burst and cut through flesh. On a failure or critical failure, if the target fails to take an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait on each of its turns, it takes 1d6 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 630 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Looking at this black fulu leaves you queasy and cold. The DC is 30, and the fulu deals 10d6 cold damage as the target’s ki warps and drains. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes damage from a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) effect or becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), it takes 2d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). These effects have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Needle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 270 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Picking up this white fulu feels like you’ve stabbed your hand with a pin. The DC is 27, and the fulu deals 7d6 piercing damage as hair, nails, and connective tissues stab inward. On a failure or critical failure, if the target then takes an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait, it takes 2d6 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Stone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This fulu’s yellow parchment feels dusty and stiff. The DC is 19, and the fulu deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage as muscles swell to crush joints and bones. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes electricity damage, it takes 1d6 [persistent bludgeoning damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 negative damage by dissipating the target’s ki. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well. These effects have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and negative traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)

This fulu’s yellow parchment feels dusty and stiff. The DC is 19, and the fulu deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage as muscles swell to crush joints and bones. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes electricity damage, it takes 1d6 [persistent bludgeoning damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 negative damage by dissipating the target’s ki. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as well. These effects have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and negative traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conduit Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4580)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Fine lines of djezet sparkle in _conduit shot_. When you Activate it, you name up to four creatures, in addition to you, that the ammunition’s magic works for. When a _conduit shot_ hits a target, which can be a square, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn’t stick to the target, the active ammunition instead falls into the target’s space, remaining active.

If you or one of the four selected creatures include the ammunition in the area of a spell that is 3rd level or lower; has an area of a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), or [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388); and does not have a duration, the djezet in the ammunition flares in striations of red light, increasing the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that has a radius of at least 10 feet or to the length of a cone or line normally 15 feet long or smaller. For a larger cone or line, add 10 feet to the length. These increases are based on the spell’s original area, ignoring any increases due to other effects, such as the [Widen Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4715) feat. Increasing a spell’s area destroys the ammunition, which otherwise remains active for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Depth Charge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4581)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved with stylized images of water or aquatic life, _depth charges_ that are fired underwater or at a submerged target function with their normal range increments and can hit no matter their normal damage type. This ammunition explodes if it hits a target underwater, dealing bludgeoning damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) according to its type. This burst doesn’t extend out of the water.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Depth Charge (I)
</title>

<traits>

<traits>

<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved with stylized images of water or aquatic life, _depth charges_ that are fired underwater or at a submerged target function with their normal range increments and can hit no matter their normal damage type. This ammunition explodes if it hits a target underwater, dealing bludgeoning damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) according to its type. This burst doesn’t extend out of the water.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dimension Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4582)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 80

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Dimension shot_ is deep blue black, but motes of light play upon it like stars in the night sky. The activated ammunition allows you to teleport to a location near where the ammunition hits. If you hit a creature, you can teleport to an unoccupied space adjacent to that creature. If you fire at a square, you can teleport to a space that contains that square or an unoccupied space adjacent to it. The teleportation fails if no unoccupied space is available to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dimension Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fate Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4583)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 80

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Fate shot_ is made of a nickel-steel alloy carved with the smiling face of a comedic player on one side, while the other side holds the frown of a tragic dramatist. When you hit a target with this ammunition, roll a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, treat all the damage dice for your attack as though they rolled the average damage +1, rounded up (for example, a _fate shot_ arrow fired from a shortbow would normally deal 1d6, which has an average of 3.5, so you deal 5 damage). This doesn’t affect additional damage dice that only happen on a critical hit, such as those added by the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) trait. On a failure, roll the damage, but your target takes half damage, and you take the remaining amount as mental damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fate Shot

[Garrote Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4584)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Wiry designs mark the silvery coating on a _garrote shot_. When the activated ammunition hits a target, it transforms into a silvery garrote that wraps around one of the target’s appendages, dealing 2d12 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). On a critical hit, it wraps around the target’s throat if it has one, and the target can’t breathe until the persistent damage ends. If the persistent damage kills the target, the garrote severs the appendage it’s wrapped around.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Garrote Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Imp Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4585)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 81

**Price** 24 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black-and-red ammunition contains an egg-shaped capsule. When an activated _imp shot_ hits, the capsule cracks open and releases a manifestation that resembles a Tiny imp that can’t act in any way or provide benefits outside those described here. If the Strike misses the target, the imp appears, makes a rude gesture at you, and vanishes in a puff of sulfuric smoke. On a hit, though, the imp harries the target for up to 1 minute, remaining in the target’s space, slapping, nipping, hurling insults, and moving with the target as it moves. A creature harried by the imp is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and skill checks. At the start of your turn on each round while the imp is active, you must attempt a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a failure, the imp makes a final vulgar gesture at the target and vanishes in a cloud of brimstone.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Imp Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lightning Rod Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4586)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 81

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a shaft of silver with a gleaming copper tip, the _lightning rod shot_ crackles with electricity as it flies through the air. When a _lightning rod shot_ strikes a target, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn’t stick to the target, it falls to the ground and becomes inactive. If the target of this shot is within 10 feet of any creature or target that takes electricity damage, the target of this shot takes 1 electricity damage for each die of damage taken by the nearby creature or target. If the target of this shot takes electricity damage from a spell or attack, it also takes 1 additional electricity damage for each die of damage dealt. If the spell or attack allows a saving throw to reduce the damage, the target of this shot takes a –1 circumstance penalty to the save. This shot remains active for 1 minute, after which it falls to the ground and crumbles to dust.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lightning Rod Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mindlock Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4588)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 81

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Mindlock shot_ is made of luminous ectoplasm in a crystalline form. When you command this ammunition, you pick a [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). If you hit a creature with the ammunition, it falls under a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect that makes it use its first action on its next turn to take the action you picked. It chooses exactly how to use that action, and if you choose Stride, the target can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) if doing so is beneficial for it. On a critical hit, the target must use its next 2 actions to do as you chose, within the same parameters.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mindlock Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ranging Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4589)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 81

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Strange striations and pits mark the head of a _ranging shot_. When the activated ammunition is fired, it sends out whistling pings along its path until it hits something or reaches its maximum range. As long as you can perceive the sounds the ammunition makes, you can tell exactly how far it has flown. The sounds are audible to creatures who didn’t Activate the ammunition, but they receive no special information from the _ranging shot’s_ whistling.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ranging Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sighting Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4590)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 81

**Price** 72 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Eyes are carved onto a _sighting shot_. When you Activate the ammunition, it glows, shedding bright light for 20 feet and dim light for 20 feet beyond that. If you shot the activated ammunition, your mind swirls with images of what the _sighting shot_ passed and hit as if you sprinted along the ammunition’s course. You see this path as if with your normal visual senses. Once a _sighting shot_ hits anything or reaches its maximum range, it stops transmitting images to you. A _sighting shot’s_ light is visible to creatures who didn’t Activate the ammunition, but they receive no special information from it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sighting Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell Echo Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4591)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 82

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Generous amounts of djezet and orichalcum mix in a _spell echo shot_. When you Activate it, you name up to four creatures, in addition to you, that the ammunition’s magic works for. When _spell echo shot_ strikes a target, which can be a square, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn’t stick to the target, the active ammunition instead falls into the target’s space, remaining active. If you or one of the four selected creatures include the ammunition in the area of a spell that is 5th level or lower; has an area of a burst, cone, or line; and does not have a duration, the materials in the ammunition immediately duplicate that spell with the same parameters. A duplicated spell can’t be duplicated again by another effect. Duplicating a spell destroys the ammunition, which otherwise remains active for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spell Echo Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4592)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 82

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The body of a _weapon shot_ is translucent and filled with quicksilver. It imparts its magic to the weapon used to fire it or it summons a translucent weapon, made of force, to fire it. It’s a favorite of killers and sharpshooters who need just one shot in a situation where carrying ammunition is easier than carrying a weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** For the Strike with which you consume the ammunition, use the ammunition’s [fundamental runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167), according to its type, instead of those of the weapon firing it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** A ghostly weapon made of force appears, wielded by you and loaded with the _weapon shot_ you activated. The conjured weapon sublimates into motes of briefly luminous dust if the _weapon shot_ deactivates without you using it or just after you use the activated shot. For the Strike with which it functions, the weapon uses the ammunition’s fundamental runes, according to its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 82

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

</row>

---

The body of a _weapon shot_ is translucent and filled with quicksilver. It imparts its magic to the weapon used to fire it or it summons a translucent weapon, made of force, to fire it. It’s a favorite of killers and sharpshooters who need just one shot in a situation where carrying ammunition is easier than carrying a weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** For the Strike with which you consume the ammunition, use the ammunition’s [fundamental runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167), according to its type, instead of those of the weapon firing it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** A ghostly weapon made of force appears, wielded by you and loaded with the _weapon shot_ you activated. The conjured weapon sublimates into motes of briefly luminous dust if the _weapon shot_ deactivates without you using it or just after you use the activated shot. For the Strike with which it functions, the weapon uses the ammunition’s fundamental runes, according to its type.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 84 **Price** 250 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Activate**envision, Interact --- A _captivating score_ is a piece of parchment prepared for musical notation, long enough for a short song. You must be trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) to properly compose this missive (or trained in an appropriate Lore skill your GM allows). You choose one of the emotions described below and write a musical composition to convey that emotion about a subject of your choice, typically a person or group. When activated, the missive plays a note-perfect rendition of the inscribed score. Each creature that can hear the score must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be influenced to feel the emotion you chose toward the subject of your composition. This lasts for 1 hour, typically returning the creature to its initial attitude unless new events have altered their attitude long-term.
  • **Positive** Your song engenders sympathy, adoration, joy, or a similar emotion. An affected creature’s attitude toward the subject improves by one step, such as from [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82) to [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=75).
  • **Negative** Your song spurs anger, sadness, jealousy, or a similar emotion. An affected creature’s attitude toward the subject worsens by one step, such as from indifferent to unfriendly.
When composing this missive, you can add a small notation or illustration of a type of instrument. If you do, the music sounds like that instrument, and if you didn’t specify, it sounds vaguely like a recorder. The choice of a louder instrument doesn’t make the music carry farther.
name: Captivating Score

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4594)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 84

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _explosive missive_ is slightly warm to the touch, regardless of the surrounding environment. When composed and then activated, the missive explodes. The tenor of what you write or the theme of your illustration determines the damage type and adds the corresponding trait to the missive: acid for a caustic inscription, cold for an aloof one, electricity for an energetic one, fire for an angry one, or sonic for an overly emphatic one. The missive deals 4d6 damage to each creature in a 5-foot burst from a corner of the missive’s space (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who rolls a critical failure also takes 1d4 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) of the same type. The missive burns to ash while releasing its magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Explosive Missive

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glimmering Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4595)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _glimmering missive_ sparkles as you compose it. When activated, it explodes, disintegrating into a shower of multicolored motes in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) from a corner of the missive’s space. Creatures in the area are covered in sparkling dust that remains luminous for 1 hour. Visible creatures can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while covered by the luminous dust; any [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures are concealed while covered in the luminous dust, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glimmering Missive

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heartening Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4596)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You compose a _heartening missive_ by creating a short message or image intended to grant the recipient moral support. You must dedicate the missive to an individual creature you know and address it to their location (typically the settlement where you think they are). Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of an animal and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at a speed of 45 feet (about 15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near the recipient or in their hand. After Activating the missive, the recipient gets its benefit and becomes temporarily immune to all heartening missives for 24 hours.

If the missive fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, it returns to its sender at the same pace, becoming non-magical when it arrives. After imparting or losing its magic, the missive remains as a normal document.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Bull)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a bull, this missive grants the recipient a sense of solidity. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves for the next 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Butterfly)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a delicate butterfly, this missive grants the recipient a sense of grace and poise. Once during the next 24 hours, when the recipient fails a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check, they can improve the result from a failure to a success or from a critical failure to a failure. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Rabbit)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a rabbit sitting on its haunches, this missive grants the recipient a sense of mischief and agility. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Reflex saves for the next 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Turtle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a square-shelled turtle, this missive grants the recipient a firm sense of self and serenity. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Will saves for the next 24 hours.
name: Heartening Missive

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heartening Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4596)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You compose a _heartening missive_ by creating a short message or image intended to grant the recipient moral support. You must dedicate the missive to an individual creature you know and address it to their location (typically the settlement where you think they are). Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of an animal and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at a speed of 45 feet (about 15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near the recipient or in their hand. After Activating the missive, the recipient gets its benefit and becomes temporarily immune to all heartening missives for 24 hours.

If the missive fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, it returns to its sender at the same pace, becoming non-magical when it arrives. After imparting or losing its magic, the missive remains as a normal document.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Messenger Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4597)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _messenger missive_ sends itself. When composing the missive, you write a location upon it. You can also include an individual creature you expect to be in that location as a recipient; if you don’t, the first creature in the location to touch the missive is treated as the recipient. Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of a bird and Flies at a Speed of 45 feet (15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near its recipient or in their hand. When activated, the missive becomes non-magical but retains its contents. If it fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, the missive burns to ash.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Messanger Missive
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Messanger Missive (Multiple)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can write up to four locations on the missive, one on each edge of the paper; you can include a recipient for each location, as well. When you finish composing the missive, it folds into four identical missive birds, each one flying to one of the locations carrying the same message.
name: Messenger Missive

[Red-handed Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4598)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Composed to look like an important document, a _red-handed missive_ is a trap used by those who suspect someone has been going through their correspondence. If activated, the missive dissolves into red dye that coats anything touching the missive. Magic in the dye prevents it from washing off for 1 week.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Red-handed Missive

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stage Fright Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4599)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 85

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Composing a _stage fright missive_ usually involves creating a scathing review, insulting letter, or embarrassing image that ridicules the recipient. The activating creature must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be overcome with embarrassment for 1 hour, taking a –1 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. During this time, if the creature attempts to speak or perform in front of an audience, they become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. When they recover from this sickened condition, the missive’s effects end. You choose when composing the missive whether it remains as a non‑magical document or burns to ash after imparting its magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stage Fright Missive

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 86

---

_Bane oil_ comes in as many forms as the bane weapon property rune. Coating a weapon with the oil gives the weapon the benefit of one type of [_bane_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1041) rune for 1 minute. A weapon can be coated in only one type of _bane oil_ at a time. Any new application of this oil supersedes any previous one. If a weapon has the _bane_ rune, this oil has no effect if it’s of the same type as the rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bane Oil

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of elemental salts and essences from the [Inner Sphere](/Rules.aspx?ID=307) or where such planes leak onto the Material Plane, _fundamental oil_ is anathema to elementals and other creatures with a weakness to elemental damage. A weapon anointed with this oil acts as [bane oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2068) to [elementals](/Traits.aspx?ID=587), but the damage type is the same as the target’s greatest weakness if the target has weakness to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. If it has none of these, the additional damage is the same type as the weapon’s damage type. These effects last 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fundamental Oil

    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"

[Oil of Ownership](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4606)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 86

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can coat objects of 6 Bulk or less with _oil of ownership_, or “red-handed oil” as some call it. Once applied, this clear oil remains active for 24 hours. Anyone who touches an object coated with this oil comes away with a red stain that won’t wash off for a length of time that depends on the oil’s type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

</column>

The stain lasts 24 hours.

right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</title>

</traits>

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oil of Skating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4607)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 87

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thin, slippery oil shimmers with a golden hue. Coating your shoes or feet with _oil of skating_ enables you to skate quickly along flat surfaces for 1 hour. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed, which doubles if you move on a downhill surface. You lose this bonus if moving on [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), greater difficult terrain, or [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370). Also, you treat any uphill movement as moving on difficult terrain while your feet are oiled and treat the results of any [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks made to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) as one degree worse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oil of Skating

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Serpent Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4609)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glistening oil has a green hue and tiny snake scales floating within. If you slather _serpent oil_ on a Tiny object that is snakelike in shape, from a stick to a scarf, the object transforms into a viper, keeping some of the same colors and patterns of the original item. If placed on other objects, the oil fails and is wasted. This false snake has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait. It remains in snake form for 1 minute before returning to its object state. If slain, the item returns to its original form, unharmed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"

</title>

</traits>

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 24 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The oil can be applied to a Small object, producing a [sea snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=807), or a Medium object, producing a [ball python](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3201).

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serpent Oil (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 56 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The oil can be applied to a Medium object, producing a [giant viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3202).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serpent Oil (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

The oil can be applied to a Large object, producing an [emperor cobra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=808).
